title: T-019 — Configurable department taxonomy seed (ADR-030) with 7 Apex depts and dept_overrides JSONB
prompt:
# T-019 — Configurable department taxonomy seed (ADR-030) with 7 Apex depts and dept_overrides JSONB

PRD: `00-FOUNDATION-PRD.md` — sections: §9
Project root: `/Users/mariuszkrawczyk/Projects/monopilot-kira`

## Goal
Migration 011 creating Reference.Departments + tenant.dept_overrides JSONB. Seed the 7 Apex depts (core/technical/packaging/mrp/planning/production/price) for the Apex tenant with [APEX-CONFIG] marker.

## Implementation contract
Migration 011-departments.sql: Reference.Departments(id, tenant_id, code TEXT, display_name, role_description, marker TEXT). Add organizations.dept_overrides JSONB DEFAULT '{}'::jsonb. Seed 7 rows for the Apex tenant per PRD §9 table. Test asserts the 7 codes exist and the marker column equals 'APEX-CONFIG' for each. Test also asserts a second tenant can opt-out of the seed (dept_overrides={'merge': {'mrp+planning': 'supply_chain'}}).

## Files (scope)
- packages/db/migrations/011-departments.sql [create]
- packages/db/seeds/apex-departments.sql [create]
- packages/db/__tests__/departments.integration.test.ts [create]

## Acceptance criteria
1. Given the Apex departments seed runs, when SELECT code FROM Reference.Departments WHERE tenant_id=apex runs, then the result equals exactly {core, technical, packaging, mrp, planning, production, price}
2. Given a second tenant inserts dept_overrides='{"merge":{"mrp+planning":"supply_chain"}}', when read back, then the JSONB roundtrips exactly
3. Given the marker column, when queried for Apex rows, then every row has marker='APEX-CONFIG'

## Test strategy
- RED: write packages/db/__tests__/departments.integration.test.ts asserting all 3 AC before the migration exists
- Run 'pnpm --filter @monopilot/db test:integration -- departments'

## Risk red lines
- Do not split MRP — PRD §9 explicit decision (#15)

## Dependencies (must be complete first)
- T-006

## Closeout evidence
Provide: changed_files, test_commands_and_results, acceptance_criteria_status (per AC), deviations_from_prd, git_status. Use the test-driven-development skill (RED test before implementation) and requesting-code-review at GREEN.
